paper_metadata.title: Application of aza-BODIPY as a Nitroaromatic Sensor

receptors.name: tetraphenyl-conjugated aza-BODIPY (4,4'-difluoro-4-bora-3a,4a,8-triaza-s-indacene, tetraphenyl substituted)
receptors.description: Tetraphenyl-substituted aza-BODIPY fluorophore synthesized via O'Shea's method; NIR-emitting at ~668 nm; central BF2-aza-dipyrromethene core with four phenyl substituents.
receptors.smiles_or_structure_notes: Tetraphenyl-aza-BODIPY (BF2 complex of tetraphenyl aza-dipyrromethene; neutral molecule C32H22BF2N3, exact mass 497.19; paper-reported m/z 498.19 corresponds to [M+H]+)
receptors.smiles_basis: Figure 1 scheme structure 4 (1,3,5,7-tetraphenyl-aza-BODIPY); RDKit-validated neutral form C32H22BF2N3 exact mass 497.1875 (HR-MS 498.19 is [M+H]+); O'Shea synthesis route
receptors.correction_reason: Original SMILES had neutral 4-valent boron (RDKit valence error). Corrected to standard aza-BODIPY chelate representation with [B-](F)(F) and one [N+] (zwitterionic resonance form of BF2 dative chelate); yields C32H22BF2N3, exact mass 497.1875, matching neutral molecule (paper's 498.19 m/z = [M+H]+).

conditions.notes: aza-BODIPY (2.5e-6 M) in acetonitrile; analyte added as 1 mM solution in ethanol; fluorescence excitation 642 nm, emission ~668 nm.
aza-BODIPY (2.5e-6 M) in acetonitrile; TNP added as 1 mM solution in water; fluorescence quenching titration.

pairwise_labels.notes: Same EtOH stock condition. TNT slightly outperforms TNP under EtOH-only condition; paper attributes overall TNP-best result to water co-solvent effect.
Same EtOH stock condition; TNP > DNT in Ksv.
TNT (three NO2) gives larger Ksv than DNT (two NO2) under same EtOH condition; consistent with pi-pi/electron-acceptor argument.

pairwise_labels.target_value: TNP in EtOH shows substantial dilution-corrected quenching (~5 units in Fig 4A)
pairwise_labels.interferent_value: NM in EtOH shows essentially no dilution-corrected quenching (~0 in Fig 4A)

evidence.quote: quenching caused by the addition of nonaromatic compounds seems to be caused only from dilution but nothing else

pairwise_labels.notes: Nonaromatic NM lacks pi-pi interaction; defeated by TNP.

pairwise_labels.target_value: TNP in EtOH shows substantial dilution-corrected quenching (Fig 4A)
pairwise_labels.interferent_value: NE in EtOH shows essentially no dilution-corrected quenching (Fig 4A)

evidence.quote: blind titrations against the solvent. As a result, quenching caused by the addition of nonaromatic compounds seems to be caused only from dilution but nothing else

pairwise_labels.notes: Nonaromatic NE lacks pi-pi interaction; defeated by TNP.

pairwise_labels.target_value: TNT in EtOH Ksv = 1364 M^-1 with significant dilution-corrected quenching (Fig 4A)
pairwise_labels.interferent_value: NM in EtOH essentially no dilution-corrected quenching (Fig 4A)

evidence.quote: quenching caused by the addition of nonaromatic compounds seems to be caused only from dilution

pairwise_labels.notes: Aromatic NAC defeats nonaromatic nitro compound.
